questions.question: What is the default value of an int instance field?
questions.explanation: Primitive instance fields are default-initialized: numeric types to 0, boolean to false, char to '\u0000'. Local variables, however, must be explicitly initialized.

questions.question: Which numeric literal is INVALID in Java?
questions.explanation: Underscores cannot be placed adjacent to a decimal point, at the start or end of a literal, or next to a prefix (0x, 0b). '1_000_.50' is invalid because the underscore is adjacent to the decimal point.

questions.question: What happens when you unbox a null Integer?
questions.explanation: Unboxing a null wrapper object throws a NullPointerException at runtime. The compiler cannot catch this because the null value is only known at runtime.

questions.question: Which widening conversion may lose precision?
questions.explanation: int → float is a widening conversion that can lose precision because float has only ~7 significant decimal digits, while int can have up to 10. For example, 123_456_789 becomes 1.23456792E8 as a float.

questions.question: Why should Integer values be compared with .equals() instead of ==?
options: == is not valid syntax for Integer objects
Integer caches only values -128 to 127; == fails for values outside that range
== compares the numeric value while .equals() compares references
questions.explanation: Integer.valueOf() caches instances for values -128 to 127. Within this range, == appears to work because both references point to the same cached object. Outside this range, new objects are created and == compares references, not values.
